citations.description: Line 97 uses get_param_optional, not get_param, for autolander-gerrit-token
ssm.tf comment describes the token as OPTIONAL, consistent with the optional fetch

findings.criterion: Credential: fetched by fetch-secrets.sh using get_param (required/fail-fast — NOT get_param_optional) so a missing token aborts boot rather than silently producing a blank env-var
findings.detail: fetch-secrets.sh line 97 calls `get_param_optional autolander-gerrit-token` (not `get_param`). The comment block in ssm.tf (lines 54-56) also describes the token as 'OPTIONAL there (blank until an operator populates this slot; the container still boots + heartbeats and only lands once the token is set)'. The AC explicitly requires `get_param` (fail-fast, aborts boot on missing token). `get_param_optional` silently produces a blank env-var instead.

citations.description: Full smoke-check-autolander.sh: no accounts/self auth check with AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN, no /a/groups/Contributors/members membership check

findings.criterion: Tests (AC3/AC5) — smoke-check-autolander.sh runs three read-only checks: (1) auth — curl .../a/accounts/self returns 200 with the autolander token; (2) membership — RebarBotNava is a member of Contributors via .../a/groups/Contributors/members; (3) ACL present — .../a/projects/rebar/access shows rebase permission
findings.detail: smoke-check-autolander.sh does NOT implement check (1) (no curl to /a/accounts/self with the autolander's AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN; it uses GERRIT_ADMIN_USER/GERRIT_ADMIN_TOKEN for all live GETs) and does NOT implement check (2) (no call to /a/groups/Contributors/members). The script instead performs: a grep of project.config for 'rebase' and 'label-Autosubmit', a live GET of /a/projects/rebar/access for rebase+label-Autosubmit, and a live GET of /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit. Neither the autolander token auth check nor the Contributors membership check is present.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
